prompt:
Scene:
A square, exhibition-ready image with disciplined composition, coherent lighting, and an immediately readable central idea. Build the frame around a second angle that tests the idea under different conditions. Keep the lighting, camera distance, and spatial organization tuned for a high-signal research image rather than a casual snapshot. The image belongs to the Fractal Generative Art theme and should feel immediately legible as a prompt atlas theme for fractal generative art, testing frontier-creative-composition with gpt image 2..

Subject:
Center the study on a second angle that tests the idea under different conditions. Translate the theme into a concrete visual statement instead of an abstract mood board: the viewer should understand what kind of image this is, what is being tested, and why the composition is difficult. Use the slug idea of fractal generative art as a literal guide for subject matter, materials, and staging. If helpful, borrow the broad visual cues suggested by Mandelbrot, Voronoi, Flow field, Reaction-diffusion, L-system, but keep the result original and specific to this study.

Important details:
The result should look intentional and technically difficult, with enough structure and material clarity to hold up under close inspection. Preserve a disciplined square composition, clear focal hierarchy, and enough secondary information to make the image feel complete rather than underspecified. The image should look polished, novel, and exhibition-ready, with topic-specific credibility coming from the way surfaces, structure, and visual language reinforce Fractal Generative Art. Avoid generic filler elements that do not serve the main concept.

Use case:
Fractal Generative Art single study 2/8, testing frontier-creative-composition through the variant study variation.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no readable text, no watermark, no logos, no generic stock-photo staging, no broken composition, no muddy subject hierarchy.
title: Variant Study
description: Variant Study interprets Fractal Generative Art as a self-contained image study with a distinct visual emphasis.

generation.runner_notes: Independent Fractal Generative Art single-image study; no reference URL required.
